Ad Text:
This is a compelling opportunity for a technology executive to lead enterprise technology, data insights capability and cyber strategy at a pivotal time of growth and transformation. This role will shape the roadmap to improve the guest experience, operational efficiency, data-driven decision-making, and cyber resilience across one of NSW's most-loved multi-site tourism organisations.


Why this Role Matters Now
A newly created role, this role sits at a genuine inflection point. Business intelligence and data insights, system integration, and enterprise architecture are all areas where the foundations are still being laid.  Reflections Holidays has a bold vision for the years ahead - increasing environmental sustainability, strengthening indigenous engagement, and improving the customer experience year after year. This is a unique opportunity to shape how technology, data, and digital capability underpin that ambition.


Leading a small and capable ICT service delivery and project team, the incoming CIO will have the mandate to embed a culture of continuous improvement where technology and data are used to drive measurable gains in efficiency and service delivery outcomes. You will establish a consistent KPI framework and deliver executive dashboards that are trusted, timely, and decision-ready.


You will build strong relationships with the CEO, Board, and Executive team, advising confidently on technology investment decisions and the future digital direction of the business.


About Reflections Holidays
Reflections Holidays are proud caretakers of the most idyllic holiday parks around NSW. Their parks are all located on iconic NSW Crown land, allowing them to create unforgettable experiences for their guests while nurturing the precious natural environment.


They give back to their local communities by reinvesting in their parks and reserves, helping maintain and protect these priceless natural treasures for current and future generations to enjoy. Managing over 9,000 hectares of Crown Land across NSW, they contribute over $100M annually to regional NSW economies.


Mother Nature is at the heart of what they do, their passion is to show Australians some of nature's best spots across NSW, whilst doing their best to protect and preserve it.


About You
You are an experienced technology executive and collaborative people leader with a genuine passion for working with the business to solve complex problems and build capability that ensures technology works for the business. You think strategically but stay grounded in delivery, and you know that the best outcomes come from earning trust across the whole organisation.


You bring a minimum of 8–10 years of senior ICT leadership experience, with a track record of developing and executing ICT strategy aligned with organisational objectives, leading organisational change and digital transformation initiatives, and delivering measurable results.


Your technical background spans enterprise systems, data, infrastructure, and vendor management, and you have proven experience in leading cybersecurity and risk management frameworks. You understand what good data governance looks like and have the commercial acumen to ensure a strong ROI on technology investment, including the ability to develop and manage CAPEX and OPEX budgets and present at CEO and Board level.


Naturally, you will have a Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business, or a related discipline.


Industry background that will set you apart
We will give strong preference to candidates who have operated in businesses with similar complexity and character to Reflections Holidays, particularly those with experience in:
Camping, accommodation, tourism, or hospitality — especially with a multi-site network of locations.
Retail or distribution businesses with high transaction volumes and operational technology complexity.
Sporting, leisure, or asset-intensive organisations with a distributed workforce and infrastructure.
Any multi-site operational network where technology connects people, places, and processes.
Next Steps
The benefits of working for Reflections Holidays include the opportunity to work on meaningful digital transformation initiatives, discounts on Reflections holidays, an attractive salary + bonus, and salary packaging options, including relocation assistance within Australia.


This position is based in Newcastle, and occasional travel across NSW will be required.  You’ll either be currently living in the area or be willing to locate to our beautiful region.   If you would like to know more, please feel free to reach out to Linda Apostolidis at linda@ntptalent.com.au for a confidential conversation or apply via the link.

Ad Text:
The Content Specialist will play a role in refining content experiences across social platforms and products. This position requires expertise in system prompting, content strategy, and data analysis. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong editorial judgment, operational excellence, and the ability to drive impactful processes across diverse projects. Collaboration, coaching, and relationship-building are key to guiding the team's success.


12 month contract
5-day working week
Working days are Monday to Friday

Job Responsibilities


Content Strategy & Evaluation: Develop and implement content guidelines and strategies for AI model evaluation, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and scalability.
System Prompting: Write, iterate, and optimize system prompts to enhance large-language model (LLM) outputs for specific product use cases.
Project Management: Facilitate day-to-day execution of workstreams, managing project timelines and deliverables at the operational level.
Process Development: Design lightweight, repeatable workflows and content templates that enable the team to move fast and iterate quickly in a dynamic environment.
Data Analysis: Analyze complex datasets to extract actionable insights and trends. Produce clear, compelling reports that inform partners and stakeholders.
Collaboration: Partner with content and product teams to improve user experience and drive cross-functional initiatives.

Skills


Critical thinkers with the ability to translate data findings into concise, actionable reports. Strong command of grammar, style, and tone.
Hands-on experience developing and evaluating system prompts for AI models.
Familiarity with social media platforms, content discovery tools, and content strategy in the context of AI development.
Proven track record managing teams and complex workflows with operational expertise.
Creative self-starter with a strong work ethic and ability to work autonomously.
Proficiency in Google Sheets and basic data analysis techniques.

Education/Experience

Bachelor's degree required

Creative Strategy Lead | Global E-Commerce brand | Health/Wellness | Fully Remote
  
Join one of the fastest growing health and wellness e-commerce companies across Australia and New Zealand which is making huge headway in the U.S, UK, SEA and UAE markets.  In two short years this company has had astronomical growth and is busy scaling its team for further local and global expansion.
  
With two successful brands, multiple markets and aggressive growth targets, Paid Social is one of their primary growth channels, and key to success is ensuring that, creatively, their Paid Social is not only impactful but delivers results.
  
The Role:
  
The Creative Strategy Lead will own creative strategy across Paid Social, working closely with the Head of Creative, Partnerships team, Media Buyers, Designers, Editors and Creator across international markets.
This role is equal parts strategist, analyst and creative conceptor. Your thinking, frameworks, and execution will directly influence growth across two global wellness brands. You should be as comfortable reading a dashboard and making strategic calls from data as you are writing a script.

Responsibilities

Develop high-performing paid social concepts across all markets for both brands.
Translate audience insights, product messaging, and performance data into creative angles and hooks.
Lead concept development from brief through to execution. You'll be scripting, writing copy, and shaping concepts yourself, not just directing from a distance.
Develop strategic, performance-driven UGC briefs across products & markets, collaborating with the Creator Coordinator to brief, review, and iterate on creator content. I
Identify hooks, angles, and video formats that are driving results across the category.
Ensure all output meets platform specs, brand guidelines, and compliance requirements
Use your knowledge of Meta to identify what's working, what isn't, and why.
Define and track creative KPIs across markets, formats, and both brands.
Translate performance signals into actionable creative iterations.
Work closely with Paid Media to align creative strategy with scaling decisions.
Build and manage a structured creative testing pipeline with clear hypotheses and documented learnings feeding iteration cycles back into strategy to continuously raise performance
Requirements:

3+ years experience in creative strategy, performance marketing, or paid social for consumer brands
Strong background in performance-driven creative, particularly on Meta and TikTok
Proven track record of developing scalable, winning ad concepts and strong scripting and copywriting ability
Deep understanding of hooks, angles, storytelling frameworks, and conversion psychology
Comfortable analysing performance data and translating insights into creative decisions
Experience working cross-functionally with media buyers and creative production teams
Experience managing structured testing pipelines with documented learning loops
Strong understanding of DTC or eCommerce growth environments (Imperative)
Ideal:

Experience working with wellness, longevity, or health brands
Experience briefing and managing UGC creators at scale
Familiarity with compliance considerations in health and wellness marketing
Performance creative delivery derived from an agency environment
Benefits:

Fast-growing company with incredible growth stats to date
Fully remote — work from anywhere
Access to great tools — VEO3, Higgsfield, Motion, Aytria, custom Claude AI tools, Frame, TripleWhale and more
Clear ownership and real autonomy over creative strategy
A no-politics, flat management structure that is results-focused
Product and health allowance every month
Work alongside team members in Australia, NZ, US, UK, SEA and UAE
If this sounds like you, get in touch today!

Why your role matters

As Manager, Student Systems (Core Connections), you will head a business-facing team dedicated to enhancing the student and staff experience through strategic use of technology, CRM expertise, and business process optimisation. You will champion streamlined processes, digital solution design, and system enhancements that deliver measurable impact across the student lifecycle.

A senior member of the Student Systems function, you will provide leadership across governance, stakeholder engagement, and technical delivery within your domain of student information systems. Working collaboratively with academic and professional staff, service units, and external vendors, you will act as a key connector—ensuring strong partnerships, effective delivery, and meaningful business outcomes.

Specific accountabilities include:

Lead and manage the operational delivery of the Core Connections student systems, alongside uplift and continuous improvement initiatives, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives.
Apply expertise in Agile delivery, business process reengineering, CRM, and digital solution design to deliver improvements that optimise the student and staff experience.
Support the Senior Lead, Student Systems (Core Connections), to uphold and communicate relevant aspects of the strategic vision and priorities of the Registrar & Student Services portfolio, including preparation of reports, papers and briefings.
Provide expert advice on the effective use, capabilities and limitations of information management systems to inform prioritisation, delivery and maintenance, while remaining cognisant of budget and resource constraints.

About You:  

Relevant tertiary qualifications and/or equivalent experience in project or program management, business systems or IT delivery, including roles such as Scrum Master, Senior/Technical Business Analyst, or Product Owner. Substantial experience leading large-scale digital or systems projects in complex organisations, preferably within higher education.
Demonstrated ability to identify and deliver service, process, and system improvements through stakeholder consultation, enhancing quality, efficiency, and responsiveness to changing priorities.
Knowledge of student administration processes and systems across the full academic student lifecycle (advantageous).
Proven leadership of cross-functional teams including technical, business, and change specialists, with strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills to influence diverse audiences.
Experience in vendor management, procurement, and service delivery, with high-level analytical and problem-solving skills, including systems thinking and strategic planning.
Experience with CRM and related platforms (e.g. Dynamics 365, Dataverse, Power Platform) or equivalent enterprise technologies, with a commitment to monitor, prototype, and engage with emerging or new technologies.
Superior written, verbal and interpersonal communication skills, with a demonstrated high level of attention to detail and the ability to engage and negotiate effectively to influence decisions and outcomes.

Brisbane CBD location

Endgame Analytics (Endgame Economics Pty Ltd) is a boutique, technology-led consultancy specialising in providing expert mathematical and economic advice. Established in January 2021, we exist to empower our clients with the right information to drive the best outcomes.

Our Operations Research team focuses on building and deploying bespoke decision models for our clients. We tackle some of the most cutting-edge challenges in energy, scheduling, inventory management, and beyond. Our team is growing, and we are looking for someone who can help us in this journey.

THE ROLE

The successful candidate will work as a Consultant within our Operations Research Team as a senior subject-matter expert. In this role, you will work on analysing data, modelling systems, and suggesting actionable strategies to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance decision-making for a range of sectors, including aviation, media, telecommunication, energy and agriculture.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Your key responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:

Managing consulting engagements, working with the team to build a project roadmap and ensuring timely completion of client deliverables.

Writing clean and well-maintained code while upholding best practices and technical standards.

Performing code reviews to maintain the quality and scalability of code.

Evaluating business needs to determine the necessary system.

Running and developing mathematical optimisation models - both linear and mixed integer programs.

Providing expert advice in the fields of operations research, data science, and programming.

Building software or working with software engineering teams to deploy the models.

Preparing quantitative analysis, building models, and assisting in developing and documenting approaches to solving problems.

Preparing detailed reports and presentations summarising modelling results, insights, and recommendations for stakeholders such as senior management or regulatory bodies.

Preparation and review of clear, concise proposals to secure new business opportunities.

Collaborating with stakeholders to prioritise key features and define a clear delivery roadmap.

Managing relationships with clients.

Effectively escalating concerns and opportunities for business improvement to senior management.

ABOUT YOU

The ideal candidate must display the following skills and/or experience:

Tertiary education in either Computer Science, Engineering or other highly quantitative degree (e.g. statistics, computational biology, etc.).

3+ years of work experience in optimisation or other relevant fields.

Demonstrated deep understanding of media, retail and aviation sectors with successful deployment of a working optimisation model in this domain, is highly regarded.

A strong background in programming and grasp of the best software engineering practices.

Experience building large scale optimisation models that are performant and scale.

Experience building optimisation models in varied domains.

Familiarity with Gurobipy, CPLEX or other solvers.

A deep understanding of Linear Programming, Mixed Integer Programming and optimisation specific Heuristics (eg: Large Neighborhood Search).

Ability to learn programming languages or tools (softwares) quickly.

Past experience working in consulting, leading projects and presenting findings.

ABOUT US

We know that our people are our greatest asset. That’s why we invest in our team by fostering opportunities for growth, development, and recognition.

Learning & Development - From day one, you’ll be involved in important projects, gaining unique and highly valuable industry experience by working closely with senior staff. In addition, opportunities to interact directly with clients will allow you to expand your professional network.

Reward & Recognition - Benefit from strong progression pathways, and opportunities to expand your role as your knowledge and experience grows. Remuneration is competitive, and will be reviewed regularly, along with a generous bonus structure based on individual, team, and firm performance.

Flexibility & Autonomy - The two go hand in hand. Endgame advocates flexibility to balance work and personal life, as well as significant autonomy and ownership of your projects. This fosters professional growth and ensures your contributions have a meaningful impact.

Collaboration - Enhance and fine-tune your skills and expertise by working alongside leading technical experts with diverse experience across industries and skill sets.

OTHER DETAILS

Job type: Full-time, permanent

Salary: From AUD 100,000 (depending on skills and experience), plus superannuation.

APPLICATION PROCESS

To apply for the role, please select apply.

Work with client SMEs and third-party vendors to ensure Dayforce configuration meets business requirements
Lead configuration testing, develop UAT test scripts and support test management activities
Support integration activities between Dayforce and existing operational systems
Deliver change enablement including business process design, training and go-live readiness
Skills and experience

Demonstrated experience delivering Dayforce implementation projects
Strong client-side consulting and business analysis capability
Experience facilitating requirements workshops and documenting design workbooks
Strong knowledge across end-to-end HCM processes including payroll and workforce management
Experience supporting UAT, testing strategy and test management
Exposure to change enablement and business training during system implementations
Ability to work autonomously and collaborate with SMEs, IT teams and system integrators
Comfortable working in a remote delivery environment
If you’re interested in this opportunity and feel you’re the right fit please click “apply” or contact Abbie Goodison (abbie.goodison@allurapartners.com.au).

About Allura Partners
Allura Partners was founded to support private equity firms by placing C-Suites to lead, transform, and scale portfolio companies. Today, in addition to our proven private equity specialisation, we’ve become a trusted partner for major ASX-listed and private enterprises in Australia, offering executive search, recruitment, and business transformation. Known for integrity, reliability, and results, our expertise spans Executive Search, Accounting & Finance, Transformation & Change, and Technology & Digital. We connect businesses and top-tier talent, exceeding expectations and driving lasting impact.

Business Analyst
Location: Sydney, NSW (Hybrid – 2/3 days onsite)
Rate: $900 per day + Super
Contract: 6 Months (Views to Extend)

We are currently seeking an experienced Business Analyst to support a large-scale transformation program for a major retail organisation.
This role will play a key part in a program focused on transitioning several operational functions to an offshore delivery model, while also preparing future operating structures aligned with a new enterprise systems environment.
The successful candidate will work closely with both business and technology stakeholders to analyse, document, and support operational and process changes as part of this transformation.

Key Responsibilities
Map and document current (As-Is) and future (To-Be) business processes related to operational transitions
Produce clear process documentation and workflows to support delivery and development teams
Facilitate workshops and discussions with business stakeholders to gather requirements and validate process changes
Secure business sign-off on process updates and documentation
Support the coordination and development of test cases and test scripts
Assist with transition activities to ensure processes and systems align with the future operating model
Skills & Experience
Proven experience as a Business Analyst within large enterprise environments
Strong experience in business process mapping and documentation
Experience supporting operational or transformation programs
Ability to engage with stakeholders across both business and technology teams
Experience supporting testing activities, including test case and script development
Strong analytical, documentation, and facilitation skills
Additional Information

Sydney-based candidates with full Australian PR or Citizenship are required.
Hybrid working arrangement.

World-renowned education provider offering a 12-month role for a motivated and technical analyst.



Your new company

You'll be joining a large, non-profit further education organisation, based in Sydney. Working in a well-established finance team, you'll be tasked with managing varied stakeholders and liaising with leadership, in order to provide strategic support using financial data.


Your new role

You'll be responsible for varied duties across the management accounting and analysis as part of a large corporate finance team, working with non-finance staff too. As the Financial Analyst, you will be tasked with the following:

Budgeting and forecasting
Month-end analysis
Stakeholder management and business partnering
Presenting financial data to leadership and non-finance teams
Influencing strategic plans with thoughtful analysis and ideas
Process improvements

What you'll need to succeed

You'll have at least 5 years of experience in a similar role, with a specific background as follows:

Strong PowerBI skills, including dashboard creation
Advanced level Excel skills
Experience in large, complex businesses such as ASX listed, higher education or government departments
Excellent stakeholder management and presentation skills
Motivated to improve processes proactively


What you'll get in return

You will receive $600-700/day + super, and you will be employed by Hays as a casual.
The contract is expected to last 12 months.
You will be working with experienced and personable teammates, and will have the opportunity to shape processes, strategies and team dynamics.
Flexible working arrangements such as WFH and flexible starting/finishing times are standard in this team.


What you need to do now

If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call Lizzie Wragg on 8062 6145. Please use job ID 2984706 in any correspondence.

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new finance position in the non-profit, education or public sector, please contact Lizzie.Wragg@hays.com.au with your CV and cover letter.

Salary: Great salary plus 14% super and bonus potential
Ad Text:
At Australian Retirement Trust, we’re shaping better retirement outcomes for millions of Australians through meaningful digital experiences. Our Digital and Personalisation team is looking for an Optimisation Specialist (CRO) who thrives on data led testing, hands on delivery and turning insights into measurable impact, bringing strong front-end development capability and experimentation platform knowledge to design, build and deploy high-quality experiments across platforms such as Optimizely and Adobe Target.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As an Optimisation Specialist (CRO), you’ll be responsible for the end-to-end delivery of onsite A/B and multivariate experiments that improve member experience and conversion across our digital platforms.  Acting as both an experimentation lead and front-end delivery specialist, you’ll translate hypotheses into robust, scalable tests that deliver insights and measurable performance.


You’ll design and deliver a structured experimentation roadmap aligned to our personalisation strategy, working closely with analytics, on‑site, and broader digital teams. This is a hands‑on role with a strong technical component, where you’ll use your knowledge of experimentation platforms and confidence in HTML, CSS and JavaScript to build, implement and QA experiment variations to production standard.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Designing and maintaining a test and experimentation roadmap aligned to personalisation and optimisation strategy.
Creating clear test documentation including hypotheses, success metrics, and setup requirements.
Building and executing A/B and multivariate tests using tools such as Optimizely or Adobe Target.
Completing hands‑on front‑end development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) to implement and quality‑assure experiments.
Partnering with analytics teams on pre‑test sizing, post‑test analysis, and insight generation.
Communicating results, learnings and recommendations to stakeholders across the business.
Supporting ideation, prioritisation and quarterly planning cadences for optimisation initiatives.
Contributing to experimentation frameworks and maintaining documentation of tests and outcomes.
Ensuring all experimentation aligns with governance and members’ best financial interests.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You have proven experience designing, executing and analysing onsite experiments, including A/B and multivariate testing.
You’re confident using CRO and testing platforms such as Optimizely or Adobe Target.
You bring strong front‑end development skills, with hands‑on experience in HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
You’re comfortable using data and insights to inform test design, prioritisation and decision‑making.
You’ve partnered closely with analytics teams to analyse results and translate findings into clear recommendations.
You communicate confidently and can engage a range of stakeholders with test outcomes and learnings.
You hold a tertiary qualification in Marketing, Digital, Technology or a related field, or you bring equivalent practical experience.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close COB Wednesday, 18 March 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

About PerData

PerData is a trusted Australian technology partner specialising in transforming complex data into actionable insights. With over 20 years of experience, we collaborate with leading organisations across corporate, health and government sectors to modernise and manage their data systems. 

Our expertise spans Business Intelligence, Data Governance, Enterprise Architecture and Managed Services, underpinned by a commitment to making technology more bankable, adaptable and secure. 

Position Overview

As a Senior Business Intelligence Consultant at PerData, you will play a pivotal role in helping our clients harness the power of their data. Your expertise will drive strategic decision-making and operational efficiency through the development and implementation of BI solutions. 

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Reporting: Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, patterns and insights that inform business strategies. Develop and maintain dashboards and reports underpinned by relevant data models.

BI Solution Development: Design and implement business intelligence solutions using tools like Power BI, ensuring they align with client objectives and enhance decision-making processes.

Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams and clients to understand their data needs, translating business requirements into technical specifications.

Data Quality Assurance: Ensure the accuracy, integrity and consistency of data across various sources, implementing best practices for data governance.

Continuous Improvement: Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging technologies such as AI, recommending and integrating innovative solutions to enhance BI capabilities.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, information technology or a related field. Relevant Microsoft certifications would be an advantage.

Experience: Minimum of 7+ years in business intelligence or data analytics roles, with a proven track record of delivering BI solutions.

Technical Proficiency: Advanced skills in SQL for data querying and manipulation. Proficiency in Power BI and related Microsoft platforms, such as data warehousing and ETL technologies.

Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with attention to detail and a focus on delivering actionable insights.

Communication Skills: Excellent client facing verbal and written communication skills, capable of presenting complex data insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Business Acumen: Understanding of business operations and strategy, particularly in financial modelling, with the ability to align analytical efforts with organisational goals. 

Benefits

Competitive Salary: Attractive remuneration package commensurate with experience.

Professional Development: Access to continuous learning opportunities and certifications in BI tools and methodologies.

Collaborative Environment: Work within a dynamic and supportive team that values innovation and knowledge sharing.

Work-Life Balance: Flexible working arrangements to support personal well-being and productivity.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer - 12 Month Max Term Contract
Ad Text:
A great opportunity to work with one of Australia’s largest general insurers

Be part of a collaborative and innovative data team

Open to candidates across Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane - Remote / Hybrid role

About Hollard   

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of Australia’s largest general insurers Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work. 

Your Role

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will work in the Data team and actively collaborate in our data journey, providing significant data engineering direction for the delivery of our Azure Data Platform. You will build and maintain data pipelines to ingest data into the Data Platform and support integration into other systems.

Your duties and responsibilities

Maintain, support, and monitor existing production Azure Synapse data engineering pipelines, PySpark notebooks, data platform lake house architecture and Azure SQL ODS storage and Stored Procedures, CI/CD pipelines to make sure all data loads on data platform meet data quality standards and business SLA requirements.

Participate and contribute to Azure data platform architecture design, data modelling, gathering and analysis of data requirements; understand document, communicate and build appropriate solutions.

Participate and contribute to the end-to-end data flow in the Azure data platform – ensuring data quality, efficient processing, and timely delivery of accurate and trusted data.

Coach and mentor data engineers on Azure technologies.

Collaborate and work closely with team members and contribute significantly to building a high performing, collaborative, transparent, and result-driven data engineering team.

About You

Extensive experience in design, build and support of data engineering pipelines in the data warehousing, BI analytics, and/or ML space is required.

Substantial work experience using Azure data analytics tool such as Data Factory, Synapse, Azure SQL, data lake gen2, key vault is required.

Proficiency in PySpark and Apache Spark with Python is required.

Experience with CI/CD using modern DevOps / Data Ops integrated processes is desired.

Exposure to Snowflake platform support and BAU operations, including query optimization, cost governance via warehouse management, and automated monitoring of ingestion pipelines.

Implemented a unified data platform using Microsoft Fabric, migrating existing Synapse pipelines to Fabric Data Factory

Experience of manipulating semi-structured data (XML, JSON, Parquet)

Working at Hollard   

At Hollard, you'll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change.

We're a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities.

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We're also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion. We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity.

We're building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we're people changing insurance.

Please note: Even if you don't meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant.

Australian work rights are required for this position.

Visit the Hollard careers page to learn about how Hollard will collect, store and handle your personal information.

We are NOT engaging Recruitment Agencies for this role and ask agencies to respect our policy of no unsolicited contact regarding advertised vacancies.

Make a meaningful difference with us:

We want people who are great as individuals, and even better in a team.

We’re on the lookout for a strategic and hands-on Solution Analysis & Design Manager, who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and lead the delivery of high-quality solution analysis and project-level solution architecture across enterprise-wide digital and technology initiatives. Operating within our Digital & Technology (D&T) Transformation function, this role is responsible for ensuring solution designs are aligned with Enterprise Architecture principles, governance standards and UnitingCare’s 2030 Strategy.

This role will lead and guide project and program analysis and design resources (including Solution Architects, Business Analysts, technical leads and vendors), ensuring design standards, guardrails and assurance processes are consistently applied to deliver sustainable, customer-centric technology outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Own the delivery of project-level solution architecture and detailed design execution for approved initiatives, ensuring solution quality, technical coherence and alignment with delivery plans.

Ensure all solution designs are reviewed through established architecture governance and design assurance forums, addressing feedback and demonstrating compliance with enterprise architecture principles and standards.

Lead and guide D&T project and program analysis and design resources with respect to scope, deliverables, design artefacts, review processes and expected outcomes.

Partner with the Enterprise Architecture Manager to support demand intake, triage and early assessment activities, contributing feasibility input and delivery insights to inform investment decisions.

Develop and implement analysis and design standards, templates and guidance to ensure fit-for-purpose, customer-centric solution outcomes.

Drive continuous improvement of technical delivery methods, reference architectures, cost estimation models, policies and standards.

Maintain oversight of solution architecture capacity, demand and performance reporting.

Prepare senior-level presentations and reports to align stakeholders and drive informed decision-making.

Analyse industry trends, product lifecycles and technology roadmaps to support sustainable digital blueprint delivery.

Drive successful implementation of D&T initiatives by translating between technology and business stakeholders.

 

What you will bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management or related discipline (or equivalent industry experience).

7+ years’ experience leading a solution analysis and design capability within complex, multi-vendor environments.

Extensive experience in business requirements gathering, high-level and detailed systems design, and implementable architecture patterns.

Demonstrated experience establishing and maintaining solution governance and design assurance processes.

Strong background across solution architecture, business analysis and/or software development.

Exceptional analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills.

Proven ability to engage executive and technical stakeholders with high-quality presentations, reports and documentation.

Experience working across SaaS platforms, cloud (Azure), integration environments and multi-vendor ecosystems highly regarded.

Relevant certifications (TOGAF, ITIL, Agile or vendor certifications) desirable.

A collaborative leadership style, strong mentoring capability and commitment to developing technical talent.

Commitment to the mission and values of the not-for-profit sector.

 

Why you will love it here:

Working with us means stepping into a role that’s as broad as it is meaningful. You will gain exposure to:

Multiple industries under one roof - health, aged care, disability, community services, and more.

A wide range of workforce models, leadership styles, and organisational challenges.

Opportunities to stretch, grow, and shape your career in ways you won’t find elsewhere.

 

And the perks? We’ve got plenty:

Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance.

Access to salary packaging, meal and entertainment benefits - up to $15,900 per year tax free as well as up to $2,650 of your pre-tax income to pay for meal and entertainment benefits. To learn more about this benefit watch the video via the following link: Salary Packaging Explained.     

A recognition and rewards platform, providing employee discounts at hundreds of retailers, including everyday expenses, holidays, health insurance, a wellbeing program and employee assistance program.

Career development opportunities to challenge yourself, grow and make a meaningful difference.

12 weeks paid parental leave and 2 weeks paid partner leave, available after 6 months service, in addition to the government scheme.

Diversity and inclusive leave, offering paid gender affirmation leave and cultural leave options (paid and unpaid) for those who may celebrate Lunar New Year, Diwali, Ramadan, NAIDOC weeks and other significant cultural events.

We’re committed to creating safe, inclusive, and welcoming environments for all—especially children, young people, and the elderly. Our services are built on trust, care, and a deep sense of responsibility.

Ready to bring your whole self to work and make a real difference? Apply now and join a team where your work truly matters.

Head of Consumer Growth

Sydney | High-Growth FinTech | Strategic Leadership Role

A rare opportunity to join one of Australia’s fastest-growing fintechs that is reshaping how consumers access finance.

This founder-led, ASX-listed business has built a reputation for challenging traditional players through smart technology, better customer experiences and a relentless focus on innovation.

 Since launching, the company has continuously scaled while continuing to take meaningful market share across their category.

With a sizeable but nimble team in place, the business sits in the sweet spot of being large enough to have real market impact, yet small and agile enough for individuals to genuinely influence strategy and outcomes.

As growth accelerates, they are now seeking a Head of Consumer Growth to lead and scale one of the company’s most important revenue channels.

The Opportunity
A part of the Exec leadership team, this role owns the end-to-end strategy and performance of the direct-to-consumer channel.

You will take full commercial ownership of the digital channel, defining the growth roadmap, optimising the customer journey, and leading a multidisciplinary team across marketing, sales and digital.

This role combines strategy, growth leadership and P&L accountability, making it ideal for someone who thrives in high-growth, data-driven environments.

Key Responsibilities
Strategy & P&L Ownership

Define and execute the direct-to-consumer strategy aligned with broader business objectives


Own the P&L for the digital channel and drive sustainable revenue growth
Growth & Customer Acquisition

Lead customer acquisition strategy across digital marketing and performance channels


Optimise the conversion funnel to maximise ROI and deliver against ambitious growth targets
Customer Journey & Value Optimisation

Continuously improve conversion, retention and customer lifetime value


Use data-driven segmentation and advanced analytics to drive cross-sell, upsell and refinance opportunities
Leadership & Team Development

Lead and grow a multidisciplinary team across growth marketing, sales and digital optimisation


Establish strong performance frameworks, KPIs and capability development pathways
Product & Market Innovation

Identify opportunities for new products, pricing strategies and channel expansion


Lead competitive analysis and market insights to support innovation and growth
Strategic Partnerships

Manage and grow key strategic partnerships, including major financial institutions and distribution partners
Technology & Customer Experience

Oversee the MarTech stack and continuously improve the digital application journey
Risk, Compliance & Operations

Partner closely with risk, compliance and operations teams to ensure responsible lending practices while improving efficiency and customer outcomes
About You
You are a commercially minded digital leader who combines strategic thinking with hands-on execution.

You thrive in high-growth environments, enjoy building and leading teams, and have a strong track record of delivering measurable outcomes through digital channels.

You will demonstrate:

Deep B2C experience across digital growth, product, sales leadership, partnerships or portfolio management


A Proven ability to design and optimise customer journeys and conversion funnels


Strong commercial acumen with experience owning revenue, budgets or P&L


Experience leading cross-functional teams spanning marketing, product and sales


Exceptional stakeholder management skills and the ability to influence across an organisation


Experience in fintech, consumer lending or financial services is highly regarded, though leaders from other high-growth direct-to-consumer businesses may also be considered.
You’ll be joining a high-performing, collaborative and ambitious team that is building one of Australia’s most innovative fintech platforms.

The culture is entrepreneurial and outcome-focused, with a strong emphasis on ownership, innovation and continuous improvement.

You Will Benefit From:

The opportunity to shape a critical revenue channel in a scaling fintech


Working with a smart, ambitious and collaborative team


A culture that encourages innovation and new ideas


Flexible working arrangements


Professional development and learning opportunities
If you’re excited by the opportunity to lead digital growth in a high-performing fintech and play a key role in shaping their growth strategy, I would love to hear from you.

Apply now or call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset on 0413 463 043.

Category:
Programme & Project Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
What you'll do  

Develop, implement and maintain the Service Portfolio strategy and implementation plan in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders, ensuring digital services meet the needs of Queensland Health and the community.
Lead service portfolio planning, prioritisation, funding, and approval processes in collaboration with the Digital Asset Strategy and Portfolio team.
Identify and manage risks within the service portfolio, ensuring compliance with relevant legislation, policies, and standards.
Develop, implement, and maintain service governance policies, procedures, and lifecycle management standards.
Provide regular reporting and insights on service portfolio performance, risks, and value realisation to executive and governance bodies.
Monitor the IT Service Portfolio and develop metrics to track consumption, quality, availability, sustainability, and benefits realisation.

About you  

We are searching for someone who has:

Demonstrated broad knowledge in current and emerging technologies, applications and “productization” of services and their life-cycle management (SFIA Skill: EMRG – Emerging technologies monitoring level 5,)
Proven ability to drive the design and development of services and/or products within a large, diverse, and complex organisation by applying good practice- including, COBIT, ITIl (SFIA Skill: PROD – Product management level 5)
Ability to provide specialist advice relating to service portfolio planning, status, performance, prioritisation, funding, and approval to inform investment decision making (SFIA Skill : TECH – Specialist Advice level 4) (NHS: Effective and strategic influencing)
Proven ability to provide strategic direction that enables the delivery of sustainable, value for money, fit for purpose services and technologies that meet the business requirements of customers (SKIA Skill: ITSP – Strategic Planning level 5)
Demonstrated analytical and interpersonal skills commensurate with managing a complex and fast evolving service delivery organisation, including the ability to communicate with clarity and influence across a broad range of internal and external stakeholders (SFIA Skill: CNSL – Consultancy level 5)

Why work with us?  

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. 

Competitive salaries 

12.75% superannuation  
17.5% leave loading 

Employee wellbeing & development 

Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members 
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave. 
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.  
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development. 

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion  

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.  

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions.  

Ready to apply? 

For further information on how to apply please review the attached Role Description.
